2.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

3.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
3.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

3.gl: properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)

5.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

6.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)

8.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

10.gl: the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
10.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);

11.gl: the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
11.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);

16.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

19.gl: blood, literally (of men or animals), figuratively (the juice of grapes) or specially (the atoning blood of Christ); by implication, bloodshed, also kindred

21.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)

22.gl: a standing up again, i.e. (literally) a resurrection from death (individual, genitive case or by implication, (its author)), or (figuratively) a (moral) recovery (of spiritual truth)
22.kj: raised to life again, resurrection, rise from the dead, that should rise, rising again

22.gl: a witness (literally (judicially) or figuratively (genitive case)); by analogy, a "martyr"

24.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
